data.title: Why a Go reader?
data.excerpt: Go runs the modern infrastructure web. Single-binary deploys. No runtime. Fast cold starts. Here's why @webhouse/cms ships a first-class Go reader.
data.content:
## The Go audience

Go has quietly become the dominant language for infrastructure tooling, microservices, and high-throughput APIs. Hugo, Caddy, Traefik, Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform are all written in Go. Cloudflare, Uber, and Dropbox run massive Go fleets.

## Why a reader, not an API?

A file reader is just `os.ReadDir()` and `json.Unmarshal()`. It's instant. It works offline. It compiles to a single static binary. There's nothing to deploy alongside it.

## What's in the package

- `Reader` — list collections, load documents, find translations
- `Document` — typed wrapper with helpers like `String()`, `StringOr()`, `StringSlice()`
- Path validation against `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$` to prevent traversal attacks
- `IsPublished()`, `Locale`, `TranslationGroup` — first-class fields

That's it. ~150 lines of Go. Zero deps beyond `gin-gonic/gin` and `goldmark` for the example app — the reader itself uses only stdlib.

## The result

Your Gin app reads the same JSON files that CMS admin writes. Editors get a beautiful visual editor. You keep Go. Single binary. Sub-millisecond reads. Static deploy.
data.author: Christian Broberg
